volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 3.4.620 - Section 3.5.676b)

sections.label: Section 3.4.620
sections.original: quid illúc clamoris , opsecro , in nostrast domo ?
sections.translation: what ’ s that noise , prithee , that ’ s going on in our house ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.621
sections.original: Núlla sum , núlla sum , tóta , tota óccidi ,
sections.translation: I ’ m undone , I ’ m undone , I ’ m utterly , utterly ruined ! My heart is deadened with fear . My limbs , in my misery , are all a-trembling ! I know not whence to obtain or look for any assistance , safety , or refuge for myself , or any means of relief :

sections.label: Section 3.5.625
sections.original: tanta factu modo mira miris modis
sections.translation: things so surprising , in a manner so surprisingly done , have I just now witnessed in-doors , a new and unusual piece of audacity . Be on your guard , Cleostrata ! prithee do get away from her , lest amid such transports she may be doing you some mischief ! Tear away that sword from her , who ’ s not in possession of her senses !

sections.label: Section 3.5.630
sections.original: Nam quid est quod haec huc timida atque exanimata exsiluit foras ?
sections.translation: Why , what is the matter — that she , frightened and half dead with fear , rushes hither out of doors ? Pardalisca !

sections.label: Section 3.5.631b
sections.original: Perii , unde meae usurpant aures sonitum ?
sections.translation: Whence do my ears catch the sound ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.632
sections.translation: Just look back at me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.632b
sections.translation: My master !

sections.label: Section 3.5.632c
sections.original: Quid tibi est ? quid timida es ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? What ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.632d
sections.translation: I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 3.5.633
sections.translation: How undone ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.633b
sections.translation: I ’ m undone , and you are undone .

sections.label: Section 3.5.633c
sections.translation: Disclose it , what ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.634
sections.translation: Woe to you !

sections.label: Section 3.5.634b
sections.translation: Aye , and the same to yourself .

sections.label: Section 3.5.634c
sections.translation: That I mayn ’ t fall down , prithee do hold , hold me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.635
sections.translation: Whatever it is , tell me quickly .

sections.label: Section 3.5.635b
sections.translation: Do support my throbbing breast , prithee do make a little air with your cloak .

sections.label: Section 3.5.636b
sections.translation: I ’ m in alarm as to what is the matter ; ( aside ) unless this woman has been somewhere upsetting herself with the pure cream [*] of Bacchus .

sections.label: Section 3.5.641
sections.translation: Hold my ears , pray do .

sections.label: Section 3.5.641b
sections.original: I ín malam a mé crucem ,
sections.translation: Away to utter perdition ; breast , ears , head , and yourself , may the Gods confound ! For , unless I quickly learn from you this matter , whatever it is , I ’ ll forth with be knocking your brains out , you viper ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.645
sections.original: ludibrio pessuma adhuc quae me habuisti .
sections.translation: you hussey , who have thus far been making a laughing-stock of me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.646
sections.translation: My master !

sections.label: Section 3.5.646b
sections.translation: What do you want , my servant ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.646c
sections.translation: You are too angry .

sections.label: Section 3.5.646d
sections.translation: You are saying so too soon . But whatever this is , tell it ; relate in a few words what has been the disturbance in-doors .

sections.label: Section 3.5.648b
sections.translation: You shall know .

sections.label: Section 3.5.649_650
sections.original: malúm pessumúmque hic modo intus ápud nos
sections.translation: Hear this most foul crime which just now in-doors at our house your female slave began to attempt after this fashion , a thing that does not befit the regulations lations of Attica .

sections.label: Section 3.5.653
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.653b
sections.translation: Fever prevents the use of my tongue .

sections.label: Section 3.5.654
sections.original: Possúm scire ego ístuc ex té quid negótist ?
sections.translation: What is it ? Can I possibly learn from you what is the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.654b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . Your female slave , she whom you intend to give as a wife to your bailiff , in-doors she —

sections.label: Section 3.5.656b
sections.translation: In-doors what ? What is it ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.657
sections.translation: Is imitating the wicked practices of wicked women , in threatening her husband —

sections.label: Section 3.5.659
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.659b
sections.translation: Ah !

sections.label: Section 3.5.659c
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.659d
sections.translation: She says that she intends to take her husband ’ s life .

sections.label: Section 3.5.660
sections.translation: A sword —

sections.label: Section 3.5.660b
sections.translation: Hah !

sections.label: Section 3.5.660c
sections.translation: A sword —

sections.label: Section 3.5.660d
sections.translation: What about that sword ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.661
sections.translation: She has got one .

sections.label: Section 3.5.661b
sections.original: Eí misero mihi , cúr eum habet ?
sections.translation: Ah ! wretch that I am ! Why has she got it ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.662
sections.translation: She is pursuing them all at home all over the house , and she won ’ t allow any person to approach her ; and so , all , hiding in chests and under beds ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.665
sections.translation: are mute with fear .

sections.label: Section 3.5.665b
sections.translation: I ’ m murdered and ruined outright ! What malady is this that has so suddenly befallen her ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.667
sections.translation: She is mad .

sections.label: Section 3.5.667b
sections.translation: I do think that I am the most unfortunate of men !

sections.label: Section 3.5.668
sections.original: Immó si sciás dicta quaé dixit hódie —
sections.translation: Aye , and if you were to know the speeches she uttered to-day .

sections.label: Section 3.5.669
sections.translation: I long to know about what she said .

sections.label: Section 3.5.669b
sections.translation: Listen .

sections.label: Section 3.5.670
sections.original: per ómnis deós et deás deierávit ,
sections.translation: By all the Gods and Goddesses she swore that she would murder the person with whom she should bed .

sections.label: Section 3.5.672
sections.translation: Will she murder me ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.672b
sections.translation: Does that bear reference to yourself in any way ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.672c
sections.translation: Pshaw !

sections.label: Section 3.5.673
sections.translation: What business have you with her ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.673b
sections.translation: I made a mistake ; him , the bailiff , I meant to say .

sections.label: Section 3.5.675
sections.original: Sciéns de via ín semitám degrédere .
sections.translation: It ’ s on purpose [*] that you are turning aside from the high road into bye-paths .

sections.label: Section 3.5.676
sections.translation: Does she threaten anything against myself ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.676b
sections.translation: She is hostile to you individually more than any person .
